edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/finemath-classifier
edges.description: The FineMath classifier is a multilingual-e5-small-backed classifier; intfloat/multilingual-e5-small is the base encoder finetuned for the math-quality regression head.

anchor_list.excerpt: A `multilingual-e5-small`-based classifier finetuned on these annotations was used to score the initial corpus.
anchor_list.explanation: README states the classifier backbone is multilingual-e5-small (resolved to intfloat/multilingual-e5-small in the lattice).

anchor_list.excerpt: A multilingual-e5-small-based classifier finetuned on these annotations was used to score the initial corpus.
anchor_list.explanation: README states the classifier base is multilingual-e5-small (intfloat/multilingual-e5-small in the lattice).

anchor_list.excerpt: A `multilingual-e5-small`-based classifier finetuned on these annotations was used to score the initial corpus. ... We finetuned a new [classifier](https://huggingface.co/HuggingFaceTB/finemath-classifier) on these annotations.
anchor_list.explanation: Source explicitly names multilingual-e5-small as the base architecture that the FineMath classifier was finetuned from.

anchor_list.excerpt: A `multilingual-e5-small`-based classifier finetuned on these annotations was used to score the initial corpus.
anchor_list.explanation: States that the FineMath classifier is built on multilingual-e5-small.

anchor_list.excerpt: This is a classifier for evaluating mathematical reasoning and deduction in web pages, fine-tuned from intfloat/multilingual-e5-small.
anchor_list.explanation: Explicitly names intfloat/multilingual-e5-small as the base model the classifier was fine-tuned from.
anchor_list.position: Model summary, opening sentence; Training section, 'Training Details' bullet

description_variants: The FineMath classifier is a multilingual-e5-small-backed classifier; intfloat/multilingual-e5-small is the base encoder finetuned for the math-quality regression head.
FineMath classifier is fine-tuned from intfloat/multilingual-e5-small as a regression scorer for mathematical reasoning content.

The FineMath classifier is fine-tuned from intfloat/multilingual-e5-small with a single-output regression head; both Phase 1 (3-point scale) and the released Phase 3 (5-point scale, score 0-5) versions used multilingual-e5-small as the initialization.
FineMath classifier is a multilingual-e5-small-based classifier finetuned for math-quality regression.

FineMath classifier is initialized from intfloat/multilingual-e5-small with a regression classification head added on top; embedding and encoder layers were frozen during fine-tuning, so only the classification head's parameters were updated.

edges.subject: HuggingFaceTB/finemath-classifier
edges.description: The FineMath classifier's training labels are LLM-judged quality scores produced by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct (3-point scale in Phase 1, refined 5-point scale in Phase 3).

anchor_list.excerpt: we used [Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct](https://huggingface.co/meta-llama/Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct) to generate annotations on a 3-point scale... Once again, we used LLama-3.1-70B-Instruct to score a sample of newly extracted pages on a 5-point scale
anchor_list.explanation: Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct generates the educational-quality annotations the FineMath classifier is trained on.

anchor_list.excerpt: we used Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct to generate annotations on a 3-point scale ... we used LLama-3.1-70B-Instruct to score a sample of newly extracted pages on a 5-point scale ... We finetuned a new classifier on these annotations
anchor_list.explanation: Annotations used to train the classifier are produced by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct.

anchor_list.excerpt: we trained a mathematical content [classifier] using annotations generated by LLama-3.1-70B-Instruct ... we used LLama-3.1-70B-Instruct to score a sample of newly extracted pages on a 5-point scale ... We finetuned a new classifier on these annotations.
anchor_list.explanation: Source states that Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct produced the per-page math-quality annotations that became the classifier's training labels.
anchor_list.position: ## What is it? (line 154); ## Dataset curation, Phase 3 (line 213)

anchor_list.excerpt: we used LLama-3.1-70B-Instruct to score a sample of newly extracted pages on a 5-point scale ... We finetuned a new [classifier](https://huggingface.co/HuggingFaceTB/finemath-classifier) on these annotations
anchor_list.explanation: Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct produced the annotations on which the FineMath classifier was finetuned.

description_variants: The FineMath classifier's training labels are LLM-judged quality scores produced by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct (3-point scale in Phase 1, refined 5-point scale in Phase 3).
Training labels for the FineMath classifier (math-quality scores on 3- then 5-point scales) were generated by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct annotating CommonCrawl/OpenWebMath-extracted pages.

Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct generated the math-educational-value annotations (3-point scale in Phase 1, 5-point scale 0-5 in the released Phase 3) over re-extracted CommonCrawl pages that were the supervised training labels for the FineMath classifier.
Training annotations for the FineMath classifier (5-point math-quality scale, Phase 3) were generated by Llama-3.1-70B-Instruct.

anchor_list.excerpt: Nemotron-Math-v2 is a large-scale mathematical reasoning dataset containing approximately 347K high-quality mathematical problems and 7M model-generated reasoning trajectories.
anchor_list.explanation: Defines the dataset that feeds Nemotron-3-Super SFT.

anchor_list.excerpt: The problems are sourced from nvidia/Nemotron-Math-v2, which extracted and filtered problems from: AoPS Subset ... StackExchange-Math Subset
anchor_list.explanation: Establishes Nemotron-Math-v2 as the upstream problem source.

anchor_list.excerpt: made by translating a subsample of SFT data from Nemotron-Math-v2, Nemotron-Competitive-Programming-v1, and Nemotron-Science-v1 into to 6 languages
anchor_list.explanation: Identifies Nemotron-Math-v2 as upstream math source.

description_variants: Nemotron-Math-v2 (~347K problems, ~7M reasoning trajectories sampled across six reasoning/tool configurations) is SFT training data for the Nemotron-3-Super-120B BF16 master chat.

Problems in Nemotron-Math-v3 are sourced from nvidia/Nemotron-Math-v2, with reference answers verified against Math-v2; therefore Math-v2 is upstream training-data provenance for the Nemotron-3-Super-120B BF16 chat through Math-v3.
Nemotron-Math-v2 is the upstream English math SFT source whose subsamples are translated into 6 languages to build the math_* splits of Nemotron-SFT-Multilingual-v1.

description_variants: Aggregator: Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 is the new collection of synthetic specialized pretraining datasets added since Nemotron 3 Nano (covers code-concepts, unconditional-algorithmic, economics, formal-logic, multiple-choice subsets); ingested during Super Base pretraining.
Leaf-level (subset 'nemotron-pretraining-code-concepts'): ~15M synthetic Python problem-solution pairs derived from HumanEval concept taxonomy, ingested in Super Base pretraining.

Leaf-level (subset 'nemotron-pretraining-unconditional-algorithmic'): ~0.2B-token synthetic algorithmic Python problems generated by Qwen3-235B-A22B and gpt-oss-120b, decontaminated against HumanEval/MBPP/CRUXEval/LiveCodeBench.
Leaf-level (subset 'nemotron-pretraining-economics'): synthetic micro/macroeconomics/econometrics MCQs generated by Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507.
Leaf-level (subset 'nemotron-pretraining-formal-logic'): NL↔predicate/propositional-logic translation, antecedent derivation, and truth-table problems generated by Qwen3-235B-A22B-Thinking-2507.
Leaf-level (subset 'nemotron-pretraining-multiple-choice'): ~3.5M MMLU-style MCQs / ~1.6B tokens, generated by Qwen3-235B-A22B and answered by DeepSeek-V3 with majority voting.

Pre-training corpus includes nvidia/Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 aggregator: synthetic specialized-domain pretraining data covering formal logic, economics, multiple-choice, unconditional-algorithmic, and code-concepts sub-corpora.
Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 is the parent aggregator for Nemotron-Pretraining-Formal-Logic (a declared subset). Used in Base-BF16 pre-training mix.

Pretraining uses Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 (released for Super3) with newly added Multiple-Choice (Synthetic MCQ), Economics, Formal-Logic, Code-Concepts, and Unconditional-Algorithmic subsets.

Nemotron 3 Super Base was pretrained on the Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 collection (released alongside the model) covering code concepts, formal logic, economics, MCQ, and unconditional algorithmic synthetic data.
Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 (newly added for Super3) supplies Multiple-Choice, Code-Concepts, Unconditional-Algorithmic, Economics, and Formal-Logic synthetic subsets to pretraining.
Phase 1 also ingests the v1.1 specialized aggregator across Multiple-Choice (w=4.0), Economics (w=1.5), Formal-Logic (w=2.1), Code-Concepts (w=2.0), and Unconditional-Algorithmic (w=0.8) subsets.
Phase 2 ingests Specialized-v1.1 across Multiple-Choice (w=4.2), Economics (w=1.5), Formal-Logic (w=2.1), Code-Concepts (w=2.4), Unconditional-Algorithmic (w=0.9).
LC stages keep Specialized-v1.1 subsets (Multiple-Choice, Economics, Formal-Logic, Code-Concepts, Unconditional-Algorithmic) in the downscaled Phase 2 backbone of the LC blend.

Several new synthetic datasets used for Nemotron 3 Super pretraining are released as Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1, covering code concepts, algorithmic Python, economics, formal logic, and MMLU-style MCQs.

The Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 collection of synthetic specialized datasets (code concepts, unconditional algorithmic, economics, formal logic, multiple choice) was added to the Nemotron 3 Nano pretraining mixture for Nemotron 3 Super and released on HuggingFace as the new pretraining data.

Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B Base pretraining incorporates Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 (aggregator of synthetic Code Concepts, Unconditional Algorithmic, Formal Logic, Economics, and MCQ subsets) added to the pretraining mixture for the Super release.
Super pretraining released the Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 collection of new synthetic datasets (code concepts, formal logic, economics, multiple choice).

Synthetic SFT-style pretraining datasets are released as Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 (Synthetic Code Concepts, Synthetic Algorithmic, Synthetic Economics, Synthetic Formal Logic, Synthetic MCQ).

Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B Base pretraining ingests Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1.1 (5 synthetic subsets: Code-Concepts 7294.5M, Unconditional-Algorithmic 195.4M, Formal-Logic 128.0M, Economics 73.4M, Multiple-Choice 1609.2M tokens) per the Super tech report.

Qwen3-Nemotron-235B-A22B-GenRM-2603 is the principle-following GenRM that supervised Nemotron 3 Super RL: it ranks rollouts during the multi-environment RLVR stage and is the reward model for the dedicated RLHF stage at the end of post-training.

Qwen3-Nemotron-235B-A22B-GenRM-2603 (the principle-following GenRM) provides preference supervision (chosen/rejected ranking signals) throughout multi-environment RLVR and the final 18B-token RLHF stage of Super 120B-A12B Instruct.
Qwen3-Nemotron-235B-A22B-GenRM-2603 (principle-following GenRM) provides RLHF reward signal across Super's multi-environment RL and the separate RLHF-only stage.

edges.description: NVFP4 release is produced via post-training quantization from the BF16 chat model with mixed-precision recipe (NVFP4 sparse-expert GEMMs, FP8/BF16 attention/Mamba, BF16 embeddings/output) selected by Model-Optimizer AutoQuantize. Calibration: 512 samples at SL=4096 from Super SFT dataset. Achieved 99.8% median accuracy relative to BF16.

anchor_list.excerpt: We apply post-training quantization (PTQ) using Model-Optimizer to quantize weights and activations to generate two efficient deployment checkpoints: FP8 (W8A8) for Hopper and NVFP4 (W4A4) for Blackwell.
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms both quantized variants derive from the same BF16 chat checkpoint.

anchor_list.excerpt: The resulting model achieved 99.8% median accuracy relative to the BF16 baseline while retaining near-FP4 performance.
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms NVFP4 derives from BF16 Instruct via PTQ.

anchor_list.excerpt: Combining AutoQuantize with the improved NVFP4 PTQ recipe produced a mostly-FP4 model, with only a small subset of layers retained in FP8 or BF16 for accuracy preservation.
anchor_list.explanation: NVFP4 inherits weights from BF16.

anchor_list.excerpt: QAD uses the BF16 checkpoint as teacher and the NVFP4 checkpoint as student
anchor_list.explanation: BF16 checkpoint initialises NVFP4 and supervises QAD.

description_variants: NVFP4 release is produced via post-training quantization from the BF16 chat model with mixed-precision recipe (NVFP4 sparse-expert GEMMs, FP8/BF16 attention/Mamba, BF16 embeddings/output) selected by Model-Optimizer AutoQuantize. Calibration: 512 samples at SL=4096 from Super SFT dataset. Achieved 99.8% median accuracy relative to BF16.
NVFP4 checkpoint is initialized from the BF16 post-trained checkpoint via PTQ + AutoQuantize mixed-precision NAS, then refined with QAD using BF16 as teacher.

QAD distillation refines the PTQ-quantized NVFP4 student using the BF16 post-trained checkpoint as teacher under a logit-based loss for 5B tokens. The same trained_from relation as PTQ — the student inherits weights from BF16 quantization and is fine-tuned to match BF16 logits.

nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-NVFP4 is post-training-quantized from the BF16 chat checkpoint nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16, using AutoQuantize-driven mixed-precision NVFP4/FP8/BF16 assignment.

NVFP4 (W4A4) checkpoint is initialized from the post-trained BF16 chat checkpoint via hybrid FP4 PTQ + AutoQuantize mixed-precision NAS, then refined via QAD (BF16 teacher → NVFP4 student) on a 5B-token SFT+RL on-policy 60:40 blend.

NVFP4 release derived from BF16 release via hybrid PTQ + AutoQuantize NAS + Quantization-Aware Distillation with the BF16 model as teacher (5B-token training budget; 60:40 SFT+RL on-policy rollouts data blend).

Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B NVFP4 is produced by hybrid mixed-precision PTQ of the BF16 chat checkpoint via NVIDIA Model-Optimizer AutoQuantize, with 512 calibration samples drawn from the SFT dataset.
The NVFP4 checkpoint was produced by hybrid PTQ + AutoQuantize NAS + QAD distillation from the BF16 chat checkpoint, with the BF16 checkpoint serving as both PTQ source and QAD teacher.
NVFP4 is a quantized post-training release derived from the BF16 release (same training data and methodology, weights quantized to NVFP4).

Nemotron 3 Super NVFP4 (W4A4) is produced by mixed-precision PTQ from the BF16 Instruct checkpoint, achieving 99.8% median accuracy relative to the BF16 baseline while retaining near-FP4 inference performance on Blackwell.
Nemotron 3 Super NVFP4 is produced from the BF16 post-trained model via Model-Optimizer AutoQuantize-driven mixed-precision quantization (mostly NVFP4 + selective FP8/BF16).
NVFP4 student is initialised from the BF16 post-trained Nemotron 3 Super checkpoint via PTQ; the BF16 model is also the QAD teacher.

description_variants: Pre-training corpus for the Base model includes nvidia/Nemotron-CC-v2: a massive collection of English web data filtered from Common Crawl. The combined v2 + v2.1 collections supplied 9.13T tokens to the Nemotron-3 Foundation corpus.
Nemotron-CC-v2 (and v2.1) — 9.13T tokens of English Common Crawl web data, including 2.5T+ tokens of organic, translated, and synthetically rephrased content — used in the base pre-training corpus.

Phase 1 (20T diversity) and Phase 2 (5T quality) draw from Nemotron-CC-v2 across High-Quality, Medium-High-Quality, Medium-Quality, High-Quality-Synthetic, Diverse-QA, Translated-Diverse-QA tiers per the Nemotron-CC quality taxonomy.

nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16 also pretrained on nvidia/Nemotron-CC-v2; the v2.1 dataset card declares the new corpora 'are intended to be used together with, not as replacements for' the existing Nemotron Pretraining datasets, making the v1 components part of the Nemotron 3 pretraining mixture.

Nemotron-CC-v2 web-crawl corpus (High-Quality, Medium-High-Quality, Medium-Quality, High-Quality-Synthetic, Diverse-QA, Translated-Diverse-QA tiers) used as a major component of Phase 1 (20T tokens) and Phase 2 (5T tokens) pretraining for Nemotron 3 Super.

Phase 1 ingests Nemotron-CC-v2 across the High-Quality (w=2.2), Medium-High-Quality (w=1.9), Medium-Quality (w=0.6), High-Quality-Synthetic (w=7.5), Diverse-QA (w=2.5), and Translated-Diverse-QA (w=1.0) subsets.

Phase 2 ingests Nemotron-CC-v2 across High-Quality (w=2.2), Medium-High-Quality (w=1.9), High-Quality-Synthetic (w=7.5), Diverse-QA (w=2.5), and Translated-Diverse-QA (w=1.0) — Medium-Quality crawl tier removed vs Phase 1.
LC stages downscale the Phase 2 Nemotron-CC-v2 component (High-Quality, Medium-High-Quality, High-Quality-Synthetic, Diverse-QA, Translated-Diverse-QA subsets) into the 80% backbone of the LC blend.
Pre-training on Nemotron-CC-v2, listed under Base Pre-Training Corpus, combined with v2.1 totaling 9.13T tokens of English web data filtered from Common Crawl.

Nemotron-CC-v2.1 — companion release of Nemotron-CC-v2 — bundled in the 9.13T foundation web data block.

Phase 1 and Phase 2 use Nemotron-CC-v2.1 across High-Quality, Medium-High-Quality, Medium-Quality, High-Quality-Synthetic, Medium-High-Quality-Synthetic, and Translated-To-English (organic + synthetic) subsets.

nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16 was pretrained on Nemotron-CC-v2.1, a 2.5T-token English Common Crawl extension to Nemotron-CC-v2 (3.8B records, 4.59TB) covering organic High-Quality, Medium-High-Quality and Medium-Quality slices, synthetic rephrases, English translations of non-English data, and a STEM diverse-QA slice grounded in Essential-Web.

Nemotron-CC-v2.1 (refreshed v2 release) provides High-Quality, Medium-High-Quality, Medium-Quality, High-Quality-Synthetic, Medium-High-Quality-Synthetic, plus Translated-To-English variants for Phase 1 and Phase 2 web-crawl coverage.

Phase 1 also uses the v2.1 aggregator across High-Quality (w=4.3), Medium-High-Quality (w=3.8), Medium-Quality (w=1.2), High-Quality-Synthetic (w=14.9), Medium-High-Quality-Synthetic (w=11.3), High-Quality-Translated-To-English (w=0.5), High-Quality-Translated-To-English-Synthetic (w=0.5), and Medium-High-Quality-Translated-To-English (w=0.5).

Phase 2 ingests Nemotron-CC-v2.1 across High-Quality (w=4.3), Medium-High-Quality (w=3.8), High-Quality-Synthetic (w=14.9), Medium-High-Quality-Synthetic (w=6.2; halved vs Phase 1), High-Quality-Translated-To-English (w=0.5), High-Quality-Translated-To-English-Synthetic (w=0.5), and Medium-High-Quality-Translated-To-English (w=0.5).

LC stages reuse Phase 2 Nemotron-CC-v2.1 subsets (High-Quality, Medium-High-Quality, High-Quality-Synthetic, Medium-High-Quality-Synthetic, three Translated-To-English variants) as part of the 80% downscaled Phase 2 backbone.
Pre-training on Nemotron-CC-v2.1 (combined with v2 = 9.13T tokens), contributing organic, translated and synthetically rephrased English web content.
Super reuses Nano's pretraining mixture, which includes Nemotron-CC-v2.1.
Pretraining corpus includes web crawl data following the Nemotron-CC taxonomy with 5 quality tiers; Nemotron-CC-v2.1 is the released aggregator for that web data.

nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-Base-BF16 was pretrained on Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1, an extension of Nemotron-Pretraining-SFT-v1 covering specialized synthetic STEM/scientific-coding/Wikipedia/math-textbook/RQA/InfiniByte slices (60.7M records, 351 GB total). Renamed from SFT-v1 to better reflect content nature.

Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1 contributes math-textbooks, STEM-SFT, scientific-coding, RQA, InfiniByte-Reasoning, and Wiki-Rewrite subsets to Nemotron 3 Super Phase 1 pretraining (and Phase 2 / LC).

Phase 1 ingests Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1 across Math-Textbooks (w=0.5), STEM-SFT (w=3.5), Scientific-Coding (w=0.5), RQA (w=0.1), InfiniByte-Reasoning (w=0.1), and Wiki-Rewrite (w=0.6) subsets.
Phase 2 ingests Nemotron-Pretraining-Specialized-v1 across Math-Textbooks (w=0.5), STEM-SFT (w=4.0), Scientific-Coding (w=0.6), RQA (w=0.05), InfiniByte-Reasoning (w=0.05), and Wiki-Rewrite (w=0.6).
LC stages keep Specialized-v1 subsets (Math-Textbooks, STEM-SFT, Scientific-Coding, RQA, InfiniByte-Reasoning, Wiki-Rewrite) in the downscaled Phase 2 backbone of the LC blend.
Super-Base ingests the Specialized-v1 component of the v2.1 umbrella (RQA, InfiniByte-Reasoning, Wiki-Rewrite, Math-Textbooks, Scientific-Coding, STEM-SFT).

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2
edges.description: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 (NVIDIA Nemotron Nano 2 family) was pretrained on Nemotron-CC-v2, the updated English Common Crawl dataset (8 additional 2024-2025 snapshots, synthetic rephrasing, English-filtered, globally deduplicated; total 6.6T tokens including organic CC, synthetic CC, diverse QA and translated diverse QA).

anchor_list.excerpt: Updated English web crawl dataset based on Nemotron-CC with eight additional Common Crawl snapshots (2024–2025), synthetic rephrasing using Qwen3-30B-A3B, filtered for English and globally deduplicated.

anchor_list.excerpt: Updated English web crawl dataset based on Nemotron-CC with eight additional Common Crawl snapshots (2024-2025), synthetic rephrasing using Qwen3-30B-A3B, filtered for English and globally deduplicated.

anchor_list.explanation: Source explicitly names 9B-v2 as a Nano 2 family consumer of this pretraining dataset.

description_variants: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 (NVIDIA Nemotron Nano 2 family) was pretrained on Nemotron-CC-v2, the updated English Common Crawl dataset (8 additional 2024-2025 snapshots, synthetic rephrasing, English-filtered, globally deduplicated; total 6.6T tokens including organic CC, synthetic CC, diverse QA and translated diverse QA).

nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 trained on Nemotron-CC-v2, an English web-crawl pretraining dataset built on Nemotron-CC with 8 additional Common Crawl snapshots (2024-2025), synthetic rephrasing, English filtering, and global deduplication; configs include High-Quality, High-Quality-Synthetic, Medium-High-Quality, Medium-Quality, Diverse-QA, and Translated-Diverse-QA.

The aligned/reasoning-tuned Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 inherits the Nano 2 family pretraining data, which the source identifies as Nemotron-CC-v2 (configs: High-Quality, High-Quality-Synthetic, Medium-High-Quality, Medium-Quality, Diverse-QA, Translated-Diverse-QA).
Web-crawl pretraining (Nemotron-CC-v2) carried into the 9B-v2 reasoning model as part of the 30% pretraining-data slice in the KD blend.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2

description_variants: General-purpose function-calling PivotRL data is released as Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Function-Calling-Pivot-v1.

NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B-BF16 was post-trained on nvidia/Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Function-Calling-Pivot-v1 as a 8.77% mixing-ratio component of the RLVR 3 stage blend (split `rlvr3`, 107,037 samples) within Nemotron-3-Super-RL-Training-Blends.

Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B is RL-trained at the RLVR 3 stage on a blend (rlvr3.jsonl split of nvidia/Nemotron-3-Super-RL-Training-Blends) in which nvidia/Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Function-Calling-Pivot-v1 contributes 8.77% of the mixture.
Nemotron 3 Super BF16 was RL-trained on nvidia/Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Function-Calling-Pivot-v1 via NeMo Gym Nemotron-RL agent data feeding Nemotron-RL-Super-Training-Blends.

Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B was trained on nvidia/Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Function-Calling-Pivot-v1 as part of the RLVR 3 RL training stage blend, with mixing ratio 8.77%. The blend (split 'rlvr3') contains 107,037 samples (4.5G).
Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Function-Calling-Pivot-v1 component (8.77%) of the rlvr3 split for Super-120B-A12B RL training; new in RLVR3 (not in RLVR1/RLVR2).
Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Function-Calling-Pivot-v1 is part of Super 120B-A12B Instruct's RLVR agentic tool-use environments inherited from Nano.

The inverseIF Adversarial v1 construction follows the Inverse IFEval methodology (anti-convention adversarial prompts that test whether LLMs can unlearn stubborn training conventions to follow real instructions), cited via the m-a-p Inverse-IFEval paper/dataset.

edges.subject: Nemotron
edges.description: The NVIDIA Nemotron family of models was trained on the Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Conversational-Tool-Use-Pivot-v1 RL dataset (170,320 trajectories, ~3.0GB) for conversational tool-use across 838 domains, with each assistant step posed as a behavior-cloning problem.

anchor_list.excerpt: This dataset was utilized in the development of the [NVIDIA Nemotron](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/ai-data-science/foundation-models/nemotron/) family of models.
anchor_list.explanation: Names Nemotron as the model family that trained on this dataset.

anchor_list.excerpt: This dataset was utilized in the development of the [NVIDIA Nemotron]... family of models. ... To be used with [NeMo Gym] for post-training LLMs.

anchor_list.excerpt: This dataset was utilized in the development of the [NVIDIA Nemotron](https://www.nvidia.com/en-us/ai-data-science/foundation-models/nemotron/) family of models.
anchor_list.explanation: Dataset card states the dataset was used in developing the Nemotron family.

description_variants: The NVIDIA Nemotron family of models was trained on the Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Conversational-Tool-Use-Pivot-v1 RL dataset (170,320 trajectories, ~3.0GB) for conversational tool-use across 838 domains, with each assistant step posed as a behavior-cloning problem.

The Nemotron family used nvidia/Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Conversational-Tool-Use-Pivot-v1 (Train 170,320 / ~3.0GB; expected-action behavior-cloning over expert conversational tool-use trajectories across 838 domains) as RLVR post-training data via NeMo Gym.

Nemotron family models were post-trained via NeMo Gym (RLVR) using this 170,320-record conversational tool-use RL dataset covering 838 domains (record fields include trajectory_id, responses_create_params, expected_action, scenario, num_unique_actions, qwen_235b_info, agent_ref, pass_rate, pass_rate_total, pass_rate_passed).

The Nemotron family used the Nemotron-RL-Agentic-Conversational-Tool-Use-Pivot-v1 dataset (~170k expert tool-use trajectories across 838 domains, posed as per-step behavior-cloning) for RLVR post-training via NeMo Gym.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2
edges.description: Nemotron Nano 9B v2 (nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2) is post-trained with GRPO via NeMo RL on the Workplace Assistant environment using the nvidia/Nemotron-RL-agent-workplace_assistant dataset (train + validation splits), launched from grpo_workplace_assistant_nemotron_nano_v2_9b.yaml. The environment grants up to 6 tool-calling steps per task with state-matching verification across 5 mock databases (email, calendar, analytics, project management, CRM).

anchor_list.excerpt: All tasks are available in the [Workplace Assistant HuggingFace dataset](https://huggingface.co/datasets/nvidia/Nemotron-RL-agent-workplace_assistant).
anchor_list.explanation: Explicitly attributes the Workplace Assistant tutorial training tasks to the nvidia/Nemotron-RL-agent-workplace_assistant dataset, which is the trained_on dependency for Nemotron Nano 9B v2.
Multi-node launch line names Nemotron Nano v2 9B as the policy and the Workplace Assistant config, confirming this is the dataset/model pairing for the GRPO training event.
Workplace Assistant resources server config wires the train split to the nvidia/Nemotron-RL-agent-workplace_assistant HF dataset; the GRPO tutorial uses this server.

anchor_list.excerpt: Download NVIDIA Nemotron Nano 9B v2: HF_HOME=$PWD/.cache/ HF_TOKEN={your HF token} hf download nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2
anchor_list.explanation: Pinpoints the exact HF model checkpoint used as the policy/initialization for the GRPO run.

description_variants: GRPO RL fine-tunes NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 on the Workplace Assistant dataset (multi-step business tool-calling with 5 simulated databases: Email, Calendar, Analytics, Project Management, CRM). State-matching verification rewards the model when post-trajectory database states match ground-truth.

anchor_list.excerpt: Responses were generated from a variety of 17 commercially-permissive models that were popular at the start of our annotation (Aug 2024). The chosen models are: Nemotron 4 (340B Instruct), Gemma (2B), Gemma 2 (2B, 9B and 27B), Mistral (7B-Instruct-v0.3), Mistral-Nemo 12B, Codestral 22B, Mixtral 8x7B Instruct, Mixtral 8x22B Instruct, Mistral Large 2, Phi 3 (Mini, Small and Medium), IBM Granite (8B and 34B) families and Snowflake Arctic.

Nano pretraining included math/code SFT samples drawn from AceReason-Nemotron-1.1.

edges.subject: LLM360/MegaMath
edges.description: MegaMath's web pipeline applied fastText lid.176.bin language identification (English-only filter, score>=0.65) to Common-Crawl-extracted documents before they entered MegaMath's web subset used to pretrain MegaMath-Llama-3.2-3B.

anchor_list.excerpt: LANGUAGE = LanguageIdentification(pretrained_lang_model='models/lid.176.bin') ... if (lang_labels[0] not in ['en']) or lang_scores[0] < 0.65: statistics.language_filtered += 1; continue
anchor_list.explanation: Code shows lid.176.bin is loaded and used as the language identification filter in the web extraction stage, transforming raw documents into language-tagged text.

edges.quality_reason: The code anchor clearly shows lid.176.bin was used for language identification filtering in the MegaMath web pipeline, but 'filtered_by' would be a more accurate relation than 'transformed_by' since the model selects/drops documents rather than transforming their content, and the subject LLM360/MegaMath-Llama-3.2-3B is not named in the anchor.

edges.subject: AI-MO/NuminaMath-CoT
edges.description: An early NuminaMath submission used deepseek-llm-7b-base (general DeepSeek 7B base, not the math-specialized variant) with Stage 1 SFT only; capped at 8/50 maj@32 on the Kaggle public leaderboard, prompting the switch to deepseek-math-7b-base.

anchor_list.excerpt: Our initial submissions used DeepSeek 7B models that were only fine-tuned on Stage 1, but we found the performance was quite limited, with 8/50 being our best score on the public leaderboard using maj@32.
anchor_list.explanation: deepseek-llm-7b-base was a design-space variant Numina tried before settling on deepseek-math-7b-base.
anchor_list.position: Section 'The training recipe', initial submissions paragraph

edges.subject: ai2-adapt-dev/numinamath
edges.description: InternLM-20B was tried as an alternate base for the two-stage SFT recipe but did not match deepseek-math-7b-base, partly due to T4 inference latency.

anchor_list.excerpt: We also experimented with applying our SFT recipe to larger models like InternLM-20B, CodeLama-33B, and Mixtral-8x7B but found that (a) the DeepSeek 7B model is very hard to beat ...
anchor_list.explanation: Names InternLM-20B as a design-space base-model variant in Numina's own ablations.

edges.subject: ai2-adapt-dev/numinamath
edges.description: CodeLlama-34b-hf (referred to as 'CodeLama-33B' in the blog) was tried as an alternate base for the two-stage SFT recipe but did not match deepseek-math-7b-base.

anchor_list.excerpt: We also experimented with applying our SFT recipe to larger models like InternLM-20B, CodeLama-33B, and Mixtral-8x7B ...
anchor_list.explanation: Names CodeLama-33B (= CodeLlama-34b-hf) as a design-space base-model variant in Numina's own ablations.

edges.subject: ai2-adapt-dev/numinamath
edges.description: deepseek-math-7b-rl was used as the warm-start checkpoint for an exploratory PPO + RLOO RL training run with code-execution feedback in Numina's own ablations; the run did not yield significant gains and was abandoned.

anchor_list.excerpt: trying to use reinforcement learning (specifically the Proximal Policy Optimization algorithm and REINFORCE-leave-one-out (RLOO) algorithm) with code execution feedback ... We applied this to the DeepSeekMath 7B RL model.
anchor_list.explanation: deepseek-math-7b-rl is a design-space variant explored in a Numina-internal RL ablation.

edges.description: Code license-detection-based filtering followed BigCode's StarCoder2 / The Stack v2 pipeline (with a stricter accepted-license list, see Appendix A). No weight or content reuse — only the licensing pipeline methodology.

anchor_list.excerpt: We performed license-based removal with a license detection pipeline similar to that used by the BigCode project (Lozhkov et al., 2024), but with fewer accepted licenses (see Appendix A for additional details).
anchor_list.explanation: Names BigCode's pipeline as the methodology source.

anchor_list.excerpt: We performed license-based removal with a license detection pipeline similar to that used by the BigCode project (Lozhkov et al., 2024)

description_variants: Code license-detection-based filtering followed BigCode's StarCoder2 / The Stack v2 pipeline (with a stricter accepted-license list, see Appendix A). No weight or content reuse — only the licensing pipeline methodology.

anchor_list.explanation: Names the VL model as the textbook-extraction transformer for STEM seed questions.

description_variants: Qwen2.5-VL-72B-Instruct extracted exercise questions from textbooks (Stemez, Open Textbook Library), with instructions to drop numbering, ignore image-dependent questions, and format equations using LaTeX. The extracted questions seeded the STEM synthetic data corpus.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2
edges.description: 9B-v2 (the released aligned reasoning model) is initialized from the depth-and-width-pruned weights of the aligned Nemotron-Nano-V2 12B teacher (post SFT/DPO/GRPO/RLHF/Merge). The reasoning model is then retrained in stages with KD (60B@8k, 50B@8k, 25B@49k, 1B@262k) plus DPO + GRPO + 0.4B@262k KD + RLHF + model merging.

anchor_list.excerpt: Depth pruning to 56 layers; Knowledge Distillation (KD) with ~60B tokens at 8,192 sequence length. Width pruning and KD with: ~50B tokens at 8,192 sequence length. ~25B tokens at 49,152 sequence length. ~1B tokens at 262,144 sequence length. Direct Preference Optimization (DPO). Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO).
anchor_list.explanation: Lists the multi-stage retraining; teacher model is the aligned 12B.

Nemotron-Post-Training-Dataset-v1 is the released SFT corpus that fueled the 12B aligned model's Stage-1 SFT (math/science/coding/etc., ~80B tokens, distribution in Table 7); inherited by 9B-v2 via Minitron pruning+distillation from the aligned 12B teacher.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2
edges.description: Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 derives from the 12B base via the alignment pipeline applied first on 12B (SFT 1/2/3 → DPO → GRPO → RLHF → Merge) and then carried into the 9B reasoning model through pruning and distillation.

anchor_list.excerpt: We aligned Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base using several stages of SFT, GRPO, DPO, and RLHF before using the Minitron compression via pruning and distillation strategy to produce the final model.
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms 9B-v2 inherits from 12B-v2-Base via alignment + compression.

anchor_list.excerpt: We have open-sourced Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 along with its corresponding sibling Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2-Base and parent Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base models
anchor_list.explanation: States 12B-v2-Base is the parent of 9B-v2.

anchor_list.excerpt: After aligning Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base, we employ the Minitron strategy to compress and distill the model... we will present the alignment process we followed to convert the base checkpoint into an aligned 12B checkpoint.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper documents the chain from 12B-v2-Base through alignment to 9B-v2.

anchor_list.excerpt: We create Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 by first pre-training a 12-billion-parameter model (Nemotron-Nano-12B-v2-Base)... we continue training Candidate 2 in an extended phase, as detailed below, to yield the final Nano 2 reasoning and base models.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper traces 9B-v2 weights to 12B-v2-Base via the alignment-then-distillation chain.

description_variants: Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 derives from the 12B base via the alignment pipeline applied first on 12B (SFT 1/2/3 → DPO → GRPO → RLHF → Merge) and then carried into the 9B reasoning model through pruning and distillation.

Reasoning-aligned 9B-v2 inherits its weights from the 12B-v2-Base teacher via the depth+width pruning pipeline (Section 4.3) and post-pruning recovery (DPO+GRPO+KD+RLHF+Merge); the pruned base 9B-v2-Base sits between them in the lineage.

Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 inherits its weights from the aligned 12B (which was initialized from the 12B-v2-Base) via the alignment chain (SFT 1/2/3 -> DPO branch / RLHF+GRPO branch -> Model Merge -> Minitron pruning + distillation to 9B). The base-model layer state in 9B-v2 traces directly to the 12B-v2-Base.

Nemotron-Nano-9B-v2 weights derive from the merged 12B aligned checkpoint, which is initialized from the 12B-v2-Base; pruning + KD + DPO + GRPO + RLHF + checkpoint interpolation produce the final 9B-v2.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: Questions from DeepScaleR (~40k unique math problem-answer pairs) were drawn into the LIMO candidate pool, filtered for difficulty, and (with newly generated reasoning chains) ended up in the 800-example LIMO SFT dataset.

anchor_list.excerpt: DeepScaleR (Luo et al., 2025), consists of approximately 40,000 unique mathematics problem-answer pairs
anchor_list.explanation: Names DeepScaleR as a candidate-question source for the LIMO dataset.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct served as the coarse difficulty filter for LIMO data: any candidate problem it solved correctly within four attempts was excluded from downstream curation, removing trivial problems before fine-grained scoring.

anchor_list.excerpt: we first applied a baseline difficulty filter using a short-CoT mathematical model, Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct (Yang et al., 2024). Problems that this model solved correctly within four attempts were excluded
anchor_list.explanation: Documents Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct as the first-stage solvability filter for LIMO question selection.

edges.quality_reason: The anchor from the LIMO paper explicitly names both the subject dataset (LIMO) and the object model (Qwen2.5-Math-7B-Instruct) and clearly describes a filtering operation where problems solved by the model were excluded from LIMO curation.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: OlympiadBench (He et al., 2024) is one of the OOD benchmarks: LIMO scores 67.6% pass@1 with greedy decoding, demonstrating generalization to olympiad-level mathematical competitions.

anchor_list.explanation: Reports LIMO's OlympiadBench pass@1 score in the OOD section of Table 1.
Cites LIMO's OlympiadBench score.

description_variants: OlympiadBench (He et al., 2024) is one of the OOD benchmarks: LIMO scores 67.6% pass@1 with greedy decoding, demonstrating generalization to olympiad-level mathematical competitions.
OlympiadBench (Hothan/OlympiadBench olympiad-level problems) is used to evaluate LIMO; LIMO scores 66.8% vs 36.7% prior SOTA.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: Qwen1.5-32B-Chat was an alternative SFT backbone in the LIMO team's RQ3 backbone ablation: identical LIMO 800-example fine-tuning on Qwen1.5 vs Qwen2.5 produced 9.2% vs 63.3% AIME24 (and 65.2% vs 95.6% MATH500), used to argue that LIMO's elicitation efficacy is bounded by base-model pre-trained knowledge.

anchor_list.excerpt: we compare two 32B-parameter models: Qwen1.5-32B-Chat (Team, 2024a) and Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct (LIMO's base model). ... We SFT both models using identical LIMO datasets
anchor_list.explanation: Documents Qwen1.5-32B-Chat as the alternative pre-trained backbone tested in the LIMO RQ3 ablation.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct was the smallest backbone in the LIMO RQ4 model-size ablation: SFT on LIMO 800 yields ~2.5% AIME24 (vs 63.3% at 32B), evidence for diminishing reasoning elicitation at small scales.

anchor_list.excerpt: rising from 2.5 for the 3B model to 68.3 for the 72B model
anchor_list.explanation: Names 3B as the smallest size in the LIMO size-scaling ablation.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct was a backbone in the LIMO RQ4 model-size ablation, with identical LIMO 800 SFT used to probe how reasoning ability scales between 3B and 14B.

anchor_list.excerpt: we fine-tune models from the Qwen2.5-Instruct series of varying sizes—3B, 7B, 14B, 32B, and 72B—using the LIMO dataset with 800 high-quality samples
anchor_list.explanation: Names 7B as one of the size points in the LIMO size-scaling ablation.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: OpenThoughts-114k served as an alternative SFT corpus in the LIMO team's data-quantity-vs-quality ablation: same Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct base, same SFT recipe, but 114k examples vs LIMO's 800. Reported average 58.3 (vs LIMO's 78.1) supports the Less-Is-More argument.

anchor_list.excerpt: OpenThoughts-114k (Team, 2025a), a synthetic reasoning dataset containing 114k examples covering mathematics, science, coding, and puzzles, with solutions following a structured reasoning format generated by DeepSeek-R1
anchor_list.explanation: Names OpenThoughts-114k as an alternative training corpus the LIMO team SFT'd on the same base for direct comparison.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: NuminaMath-100k (a 100k random subset of NuminaMath-CoT) served as an alternative SFT corpus in the LIMO team's data-quantity-vs-quality ablation: same Qwen2.5-32B-Instruct base, same SFT recipe, 100k examples vs LIMO's 800. Average 32.3 (well below LIMO's 78.1) supports the Less-Is-More argument.

anchor_list.excerpt: NuminaMath-100k, a randomly selected 100k subset of NuminaMath-CoT, featuring mathematical problems ranging from Chinese high school exercises to international mathematics olympiad competitions
anchor_list.explanation: Names NuminaMath-100k as an alternative training corpus the LIMO team SFT'd on the same base for direct comparison.

edges.subject: GAIR/LIMO
edges.description: LIMO's data-efficiency thesis (high-quality minimal exemplars elicit complex behavior) is an explicit extension of LIMA's 'Less is More for Alignment' approach (1,000 curated prompts) from instruction-following to mathematical reasoning. No weight or data inheritance — methodological lineage only.

anchor_list.excerpt: Our work extends the ideology of LIMA to reasoning tasks by investigating what constitutes high-quality questions and solutions, and demonstrates that the reasoning ability of LLMs can be enhanced in a highly data-efficient manner.
anchor_list.explanation: Explicitly identifies LIMA as the methodological inspiration LIMO extends to reasoning.

edges.subject: Goedel-LM/Goedel-Prover-V2-32B
edges.description: Goedel-Prover-V2-32B's prompt config (lean4/goedel-prover-v2) is the basis for the goedel-prover-v2-nemotron prompt used in the Nemotron-Nano-v3 miniF2F evaluation, and Goedel-Prover-V2-32B serves as a baseline for self-correction-style proof generation referenced in the recipe.

anchor_list.explanation: The miniF2F eval uses a Goedel-Prover-V2-derived prompt; Goedel-Prover-V2-32B was the proof generator producing miniF2F-style training trajectories.

anchor_list.excerpt: Our flagship model, Goedel-Prover-V2-32B, achieves 88.0% on MiniF2F at Pass@32 on standard mode and 90.4% on self-correction mode
anchor_list.explanation: Quotes the 32B model's specific MiniF2F Pass@32 numbers, confirming MiniF2F is used to evaluate this release.
anchor_list.position: Section 1 Introduction, sentence beginning 'Our flagship model'

anchor_list.excerpt: We use the version of MiniF2F provided by Kimina, which has some incorrect statements fixed.
anchor_list.explanation: Confirms MiniF2F as a primary evaluation benchmark for the 32B prover.

anchor_list.excerpt: Our flagship model, Goedel-Prover-V2-32B, achieves 88.0% on MiniF2F at Pass@32 on standard mode and 90.4% on self-correction mode
anchor_list.explanation: Reports MiniF2F as a primary evaluation benchmark for Goedel-Prover-V2-32B.

description_variants: Goedel-Prover-V2-32B is evaluated on the miniF2F test set, achieving 88.0% Pass@32 in standard mode and 90.4% Pass@32 in self-correction mode (Figure 1 / Figure 2 also report scaling curves on miniF2F).

Goedel-Prover-V2-32B is evaluated on MiniF2F (488 problems; the team uses the AI-MO/minif2f_test variant with statement fixes from Kimina). It reaches 88.1% pass@32 standard and 90.4% with two rounds of self-correction.

Goedel-Prover-V2-32B is evaluated on the MiniF2F test set at Pass@32 (88.0% standard mode, 90.4% self-correction mode); the released repo bundles the MiniF2F test problems in dataset/minif2f.jsonl as the standard inference input.

edges.subject: nvidia/AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B
edges.description: AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B is supervised fine-tuned on the released nvidia/AceReason-1.1-SFT dataset, which combines ~247K math and ~136K code prompts (final v7 expanded to ~1.2M math + 1.0M code samples) with DeepSeek-R1-generated responses, after dedup and 9-gram decontamination against math/coding benchmark test samples.

anchor_list.excerpt: This resulted in a final dataset of 247K math prompts and 136K code prompts, totaling 383K prompts. ... We release the model and data at: https://huggingface.co/nvidia/AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B
anchor_list.explanation: Paper describes the SFT mixture quantitatively and explicitly releases the dataset alongside the model.
Sub-bullets under the 1.1-7B release announcement directly link the 4M SFT dataset (nvidia/AceReason-1.1-SFT) to the 1.1-7B model.

anchor_list.excerpt: It serves as the SFT training data for AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B
anchor_list.explanation: Dataset card explicitly states the consumer model is AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B.

description_variants: AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B is supervised fine-tuned on the released nvidia/AceReason-1.1-SFT dataset, which combines ~247K math and ~136K code prompts (final v7 expanded to ~1.2M math + 1.0M code samples) with DeepSeek-R1-generated responses, after dedup and 9-gram decontamination against math/coding benchmark test samples.

AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B uses nvidia/AceReason-1.1-SFT (4M math+code SFT samples) as the SFT-stage training data before its subsequent RL stage; the dataset is the 'SFT' half of the 'combining SFT with RL' recipe announced for the 1.1-7B release.
AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B's SFT stage uses the AceReason-1.1-SFT dataset (3,970,332 samples = 2.67M math + 1.30M code) as its supervised fine-tuning data.

edges.subject: nvidia/AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B

anchor_list.explanation: AceMath dataset is named as a math prompt source for SFT training data.

edges.subject: nvidia/AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B
edges.description: AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B reuses the math RL training dataset from AceReason-Nemotron-1.0, namely nvidia/AceReason-Math (~49K math problems sourced from NuminaMath-1.5 and DeepScaleR-Preview, with sub-question/MCQ/proof filtering). Used across Math-only Stage-1 (8K), Stage-2 (16K), Stage-3 (24K) and Stage-4 (32K) with progressive difficulty filtering (e.g. Stage-3 keeps ~2,500 hard problems).

anchor_list.excerpt: We utilize the high-quality math and code RL data from AceReason-Nemotron-1.0 (Chen et al., 2025). ... For each stage, we use the same RL training dataset as AceReason-Nemotron-1.0.

description_variants: AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B reuses the math RL training dataset from AceReason-Nemotron-1.0, namely nvidia/AceReason-Math (~49K math problems sourced from NuminaMath-1.5 and DeepScaleR-Preview, with sub-question/MCQ/proof filtering). Used across Math-only Stage-1 (8K), Stage-2 (16K), Stage-3 (24K) and Stage-4 (32K) with progressive difficulty filtering (e.g. Stage-3 keeps ~2,500 hard problems).

AceReason-Math (49K verifiable math problems sourced from NuminaMath-1.5 and DeepScaleR-Preview) is the math RL training dataset released alongside AceReason-Nemotron-1.1-7B as one of the official AceReason 1.1 artifacts; the model card surfaces it as the 'Math RL Dataset' for this release.
edges.quality_reason: Two strong anchors: the paper states 'We utilize the high-quality math and code RL data from AceReason-Nemotron-1.0', and the model card badge links AceReason-Math as the 'Math RL Dataset'.

edges.subject: allenai/WildChat-1M
edges.description: OpenAI's omni-moderation-latest is invoked on every WildChat turn (utils.py:query_moderation) and its category outputs decide inclusion: the Full release excludes 'sexual/minors'-flagged conversations, and the standard (non-Full) release additionally excludes any conversation where moderation['flagged'] is True or detoxify toxicity exceeds 0.1. WildLlama (and any model fine-tuned on WildChat) therefore consumes a corpus whose composition is filtered by omni-moderation-latest decisions.

anchor_list.explanation: Names omni-moderation-latest as the model running per-turn moderation in WildChat construction.
release-time filter directly uses omni-moderation-latest's category outputs to drop WildChat conversations from both Full and standard releases.
anchor_list.position: no_minors filter at lines 1036-1051 in push_dataset

anchor_list.explanation: Pins the moderation classifier to OpenAI's omni-moderation-latest model, exact match for the OpenAI/omni-moderation-latest lattice leaf.

anchor_list.excerpt: if turn['content'] != '' and (turn['openai_moderation']['flagged'] or ...): turn_toxic = True ... ds = ds.filter(lambda ex: not ex["toxic"] ...) ... repo_id = f"allenai/WildChat-{repo_size_str}"
anchor_list.explanation: Documents the `flagged` field driving exclusion of conversations from the public WildChat release — the filtering decision.

description_variants: The omni-moderation-latest model labels every WildChat conversation turn; turns whose response carries flagged=True (or whose conversation contains any flagged turn) are excluded from the public allenai/WildChat-4.8M release (kept only in the gated -Full release). The model only decides inclusion — content is unchanged — which is the filtered_by signature.
edges.quality_reason: The anchors clearly establish omni-moderation-latest filtering the WildChat corpus, but WildLlama is never named in any anchor; the dependency is inferred indirectly from WildLlama training on WildChat, making the subject-object link one step removed from the evidence.

edges.subject: allenai/WildChat-1M
edges.description: WildChat content in English is processed by the en_core_web_trf spaCy NER pipeline (configured in run_presidio_ner.py and consumed by Microsoft Presidio's AnalyzerEngine) to identify entity spans (PERSON, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PHONE_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD, URL, IP_ADDRESS, etc.). Spans surviving frequency / context filters are then anonymized in-place to placeholders like <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON> by redact_PII.py, transforming the released WildChat text. WildLlama (and any model trained on WildChat) consumes this redacted variant of the content.

anchor_list.explanation: Source explicitly registers the en_core_web_trf spaCy pipeline as the English NER backbone for Presidio's AnalyzerEngine in the PII redaction pass.

anchor_list.excerpt:
result = anonymizer.anonymize(
    text=text_to_anonymize,
    analyzer_results=analyzer_results_new,
    operators=operators
)
turn["content"] = result.text
turn['redacted'] = True
anchor_list.explanation: PII spans identified by spaCy-backed Presidio analyzer are replaced in-place, transforming WildChat content before release.
anchor_list.position: function main, lines 195-296 (entity loop and anonymizer.anonymize call)

English PII spans in WildChat were detected by the English spaCy transformer pipeline (en_core_web_trf) and redacted before WildLlama saw the conversations. English is the dominant language in WildChat (~53% of turns; Table 5).

en_core_web_trf (English transformer-based spaCy pipeline on roberta-base) is loaded in the Presidio NLP engine for English (lang_code=English) and runs NER over English-language WildChat turns; detected PERSON / EMAIL / PHONE / CREDIT_CARD / URL / etc. spans drive the AnonymizerEngine to replace them with <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_*> placeholders, modifying the released turn content.
edges.quality_reason: The anchors confirm en_core_web_trf is used for PII redaction of English WildChat content, but WildLlama is not named in any anchor; the transformation applies to the WildChat dataset and WildLlama's dependency is an indirect inference from training on that corpus.

Spans surviving frequency / context filters are then anonymized in-place to placeholders like <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON> by redact_PII.py, transforming the released WildChat text. WildLlama (and any model trained on WildChat) consumes this redacted variant of the content.

anchor_list.explanation: Source explicitly registers the de_core_news_lg spaCy pipeline as the German NER backbone for Presidio's AnalyzerEngine in the PII redaction pass.

anchor_list.excerpt:
result = anonymizer.anonymize(
    text=text_to_anonymize,
    analyzer_results=analyzer_results_new,
    operators=operators
)
turn["content"] = result.text
turn['redacted'] = True
anchor_list.explanation: PII spans identified by spaCy-backed Presidio analyzer are replaced in-place, transforming WildChat content before release.
anchor_list.position: function main, lines 195-296 (entity loop and anonymizer.anonymize call)

edges.subject: allenai/WildChat-1M
edges.description: WildBench (allenai/WildBench) is the held-out evaluation benchmark for WildChat-trained models: utils.remove_wildbench loads wildbench_ids.json and excises every conversation reserved for WildBench from the WildChat training corpus before release. This decontamination is precisely the held-out role of an evaluation set, and WildLlama (the WildChat-fine-tuned Llama-2 family in the lattice) is the corresponding model whose training corpus is held out from WildBench.

anchor_list.excerpt:
wildbench_ids = json.load(open('wildbench_ids.json'))
...
for example in wildbench_ids:
    first_50char = example['conversation_1st_turn_50chars']
    if first_50char not in first_50char_to_id:
        ...
    ids_to_remove.extend(ids)
anchor_list.explanation: Source code demonstrates that conversations reserved for WildBench are excluded from WildChat, establishing WildBench's held-out evaluation role for WildChat-trained models.

anchor_list.excerpt: remove_wildbench: removes conversations reserved for WildBench.

edges.subject: nvidia/NVIDIA-Nemotron-3-Nano-30B-A3B-Base-BF16
edges.description: Hybrid Mamba-Transformer macro-architecture is inherited from prior Nemotron-H and Nemotron 2 Nano designs (concept-level reference; no released checkpoint of either is in the lattice).

anchor_list.excerpt: Nemotron 3 Nano 30B-A3B Base builds upon the hybrid Mamba-Transformer architecture of our older Nemotron-H ... and Nemotron 2 Nano ... models by replacing the standard FFN layers with sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) ... layers.
anchor_list.explanation: Paper directly attributes the hybrid Mamba-Transformer architecture to the prior Nemotron-H and Nemotron 2 Nano models.

anchor_list.excerpt: Similar to Nemotron 2 Nano, we added a long-context phase (LC-Phase) at the end of pretraining ... We reused the long-context document QA dataset from Nemotron Nano 2, but scaled it to make it 3x larger.
anchor_list.explanation: Long-context recipe and dataset are inherited from Nemotron Nano 2.

description_variants: Hybrid Mamba-Transformer macro-architecture is inherited from prior Nemotron-H and Nemotron 2 Nano designs (concept-level reference; no released checkpoint of either is in the lattice).

Long-context CPT reuses the Nemotron Nano 2 long-context document-QA dataset (scaled 3x) and follows the Nemotron Nano 2 LC-Phase recipe; the Nemotron Nano 2 dataset is not separately released, so this is captured as concept-level inheritance from the Nemotron family.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct
edges.description: Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct is the chat-aligned post-training of the 340B Base checkpoint, produced in NeMo-Aligner alongside the Reward variant.

anchor_list.excerpt: We released Nemotron-4-340B [Base](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Base), [Instruct](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct), [Reward](https://huggingface.co/nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Reward). The Instruct and Reward variants are trained in Nemo-Aligner.
anchor_list.explanation: README explicitly groups Base/Instruct/Reward and notes that the Instruct variant is trained in NeMo-Aligner from the released Base.

anchor_list.excerpt: We released Nemotron-4-340B Base, Instruct, Reward. The Instruct and Reward variants are trained in Nemo-Aligner.
anchor_list.explanation: Co-release of Base + Instruct with the Instruct described as 'trained' in NeMo-Aligner implies post-training from the Base.

anchor_list.excerpt: It is a fine-tuned version of the Nemotron-4-340B-Base model, optimized for English-based single and multi-turn chat use-cases.

description_variants: Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct is the chat-aligned post-training of the 340B Base checkpoint, produced in NeMo-Aligner alongside the Reward variant.

Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct is initialized from the Nemotron-4-340B-Base checkpoint and then aligned via SFT, DPO and RPO.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct
edges.description: Synthetic prompts (open Q&A, writing, closed Q&A, math&coding, instruction-following, two-turn) used in Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct's SFT and preference data are generated by Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1, the initial aligned model in iter-1 weak-to-strong alignment.

anchor_list.excerpt: we use the permissive Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1 (Jiang et al., 2024) as our generator to generate synthetic prompts separately for the tasks including open Q&A, closed Q&A, math&coding.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct
edges.description: Closed-Q&A synthetic prompts are seeded from documents in the C4 corpus (Raffel et al. 2020); each document is concatenated with a generated instruction (e.g., 'summarize the given text') to form a closed-Q&A prompt used in SFT data.

anchor_list.excerpt: We use the texts in the C4 dataset (Raffel et al., 2020) for generating closed Q&A prompts. For each given document, we ask the generator to output respected instructions.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct
edges.description: First user prompts of synthetic two-turn prompts (used in preference fine-tuning data) are sourced from ShareGPT (RyokoAI, 2023); the assistant and next-user turns are then synthesized by intermediate instruct models.

anchor_list.excerpt: We source the first user prompts from ShareGPT (RyokoAI, 2023), and generate the assistant response and the next turn question with our intermediate instruct models.
anchor_list.explanation: Names ShareGPT (RyokoAI release) as the seed for first-user turns.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct
edges.description: Real-world prompts drawn from LMSYS-Chat-1M (Zheng et al. 2023) are split into two non-overlapping sets used for SFT and preference learning respectively; the SFT split removes prompts flagged as unsafe, the preference split retains them.

anchor_list.excerpt: we also draw prompts from LMSYS-Chat-1M (LMSYS) (Zheng et al., 2023). We combine all prompts in a balanced ratio and divide them into two distinct sets, one for supervised learning and another for preference learning.
anchor_list.explanation: Names LMSYS-Chat-1M as the real-world prompt source for both SFT and preference data.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct
edges.description: The synthetic-prompt diversity recipe (macro topic / subtopic / writing-task structure) follows the UltraChat dataset construction methodology (Ding et al. 2023).

anchor_list.excerpt: we adopt a similar approach to the generation of the UltraChat dataset (Ding et al., 2023) and CAMEL (Li et al., 2023).
anchor_list.explanation: Names UltraChat as a recipe inspiration.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct
edges.description: The synthetic-prompt diversity recipe also follows CAMEL (Li et al. 2023), specifically the practice of seeding role-play / topical conversations from synthetic topics and document types.

anchor_list.excerpt: we adopt a similar approach to the generation of the UltraChat dataset (Ding et al., 2023) and CAMEL (Li et al., 2023).
anchor_list.explanation: Names CAMEL as a recipe inspiration.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct
edges.description: Nemotron-4-340B-Reward scores each three-turn synthetic dialogue and filters out samples below a quality threshold; surviving high-quality dialogues become Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct's SFT training data.

anchor_list.excerpt: we utilize Nemotron-4-340B-Reward to assess the quality of dialogues, assigning a score to each sample and filtering out those that fall below a predetermined threshold.
anchor_list.explanation: States the reward model is the judge filter for SFT dialogue data.

anchor_list.excerpt: we further explored Reward-Model-as-Judge, where we ask Nemotron-4-340B-Reward to predict the reward for each (prompt, response) pair and decide the preference ranking based on the rewards.
anchor_list.explanation: Names Nemotron-4-340B-Reward as the preference judge feeding DPO/RPO training data.

anchor_list.excerpt: we use Nemotron-4-340B-Reward to pick examples with high-quality chosen responses when the ground-truth is not available. This leads to a preference dataset with 160K examples.
anchor_list.explanation: States the reward model is the filter that selects DPO training examples.

anchor_list.excerpt: the majority of our preference data are synthetic, whose preference rank is judged according to the reward from Nemotron-4-340B-Reward. ... RPO learns to approximate the reward gap.
anchor_list.explanation: States the reward model is the source of preference rank and reward gap RPO uses.

anchor_list.excerpt: developers can use the Nemotron-4 340B Reward model to filter for high-quality responses. Nemotron-4 340B Reward grades responses on five attributes: helpfulness, correctness, coherence, complexity and verbosity.
anchor_list.explanation: Restates the Reward model as the global filter in the pipeline that produced 340B-Instruct's training data.
anchor_list.position: Blog body 'Navigating Nemotron to Generate Synthetic Data'

anchor_list.excerpt: The Instruct and Reward variants are trained in Nemo-Aligner. Please see the [Helpsteer2](https://arxiv.org/abs/2406.08673) paper for more details on the reward model training.
anchor_list.explanation: Co-release framing pairs the Instruct policy with the Reward model; the HelpSteer2 reference establishes the RM-driven alignment recipe.

description_variants: Nemotron-4-340B-Reward scores each three-turn synthetic dialogue and filters out samples below a quality threshold; surviving high-quality dialogues become Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct's SFT training data.

In later preference-data iterations, Nemotron-4-340B-Reward serves as Reward-Model-as-Judge — scoring (prompt, response) pairs and deciding preference rank. It replaced LLM-as-Judge after observing higher Chat-Hard accuracy on RewardBench (0.87 vs 0.54).

Nemotron-4-340B-Reward filters which preference triplets enter DPO training: it picks examples whose chosen-response quality is high when ground-truth labels (e.g., GSM8K/MATH answers, IF python verifier) are unavailable. Resulting DPO dataset is 160K examples.

RPO uses Nemotron-4-340B-Reward both to filter the 300K-example preference dataset (less harsh chosen-quality filter than DPO) and to compute the reward gap r*(x,y_c) - r*(x,y_l) that the RPO loss approximates.

Across the entire alignment pipeline (synthetic-dialogue quality filter §3.2.2, preference-data Reward-Model-as-Judge §3.2.3, DPO chosen-response selection §3.3.2, RPO 300K-example filter §3.3.2), Nemotron-4-340B-Reward is the global judge / quality filter that decides which synthetic samples enter Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct training.
The companion 340B reward model in the same release scores responses to drive RLHF/RM-guided alignment of the Instruct variant.
edges.quality_reason: Multiple anchors clearly state that Nemotron-4-340B-Reward scores and filters training dialogues and preference data, directly establishing a filtered_by relationship for the Instruct model's training pipeline.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct
edges.description: Final-iteration synthetic SFT dialogues are generated by 340B-Interm-2-Chat (the iter-2 aligned model) using greedy sampling; this is the data generator for Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct's last alignment iteration before final SFT.

anchor_list.excerpt: Throughout the entire alignment procedure, we conduct multiple rounds of data generation and refinement, continually improving the quality of our models.
anchor_list.explanation: Establishes that later-iteration intermediate models become the data generators feeding the final SFT data.

anchor_list.excerpt: we also build more challenging synthetic preference examples, when the responses are multiple random generations from our best-performing model according to MT-Bench. These challenging preference examples enable our model to further improve itself.
anchor_list.explanation: Identifies the best-performing intermediate model as a response generator in preference data.

description_variants: Final-iteration synthetic SFT dialogues are generated by 340B-Interm-2-Chat (the iter-2 aligned model) using greedy sampling; this is the data generator for Nemotron-4-340B-Instruct's last alignment iteration before final SFT.

Multiple random intermediate-model responses are sampled per prompt to form preference pairs; the strongest generator at the time (340B-Interm-2-Chat in the final iteration) also seeds challenging preference examples per MT-Bench.

anchor_list.excerpt: Pass@32 performance on MiniF2F, PutnamBench, and our new MathOlympiadBench containing 360 IMO-level problems.

description_variants: Goedel-Prover-V2-32B is evaluated on MathOlympiadBench (360 IMO-level problems sourced from Compfiles and IMOSLLean4); Pass@32 results are reported alongside MiniF2F and PutnamBench in Figure 1.
Goedel-Prover-V2-32B is evaluated on MathOlympiadBench (360 IMO-level formalized problems sourced from Compfiles and IMOSLLean4). It solves 73 problems with self-correction at pass@32 (Figure 1).

Goedel-Prover-V2-32B is evaluated on MathOlympiadBench, a 360-problem Lean 4 Olympiad benchmark (158 IMO 1959-2024, 131 IMO shortlist 2006-2023, 68 national Olympiads, 3 puzzles) introduced alongside the V2 release. Each problem is human-verified to contain exactly one formal theorem matching its informal statement and to compile with the sorry tactic against current Mathlib.
Goedel-Prover-V2-32B is evaluated at Pass@32 on the team's newly released MathOlympiadBench, a 360-problem IMO-level Lean 4 benchmark introduced in the same release.

anchor_list.explanation: Same prompt-source provenance for the 340B reward model that also trained on HelpSteer2.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Reward
edges.description: Same FastText language filter applied to the prompts seen by the Nemotron-4 340B Reward model (which trained on HelpSteer2).

anchor_list.excerpt: we removed all non-English prompts, as identified by FastText.
anchor_list.explanation: Inherited filtering for the 340B reward model.

edges.quality_reason: The anchor confirms FastText filtered non-English prompts from HelpSteer2, but neither the subject 'Nemotron-4-340B-Reward' nor its connection to HelpSteer2 is named in the excerpt, making this an inferred inherited dependency.

edges.subject: nvidia/Nemotron-4-340B-Reward
edges.description: Same Nemotron-2-43B complexity filter applied upstream of the 340B Reward model's HelpSteer2 training data.

anchor_list.excerpt: we assessed the complexity of each prompt on a Likert-5 scale using Nemotron-2-43B.
anchor_list.explanation: Inherited complexity filter for the 340B RM.

edges.quality_reason: The anchor confirms Nemotron-2-43B scored prompt complexity, but this describes annotation/weighting rather than hard filtering, and the subject 'Nemotron-4-340B-Reward' is not mentioned in the excerpt.

edges.subject: Nemotron-3
edges.description: The 22B Nemotron-3 multi-turn-completion model's instruction-following SFT blend included OpenAssistant Conversations.

anchor_list.excerpt: fine-tuned for instruction following on a dataset blend of 128K samples that come in particular from Helpsteer [12], Open Assistant [11].
anchor_list.explanation: OpenAssistant Conversations is in the SFT blend.

anchor_list.excerpt: the Reward Model is trained on a preference dataset based on Open Assistant [11].
anchor_list.explanation: Open Assistant supplies the preferences used by the PPO reward model.

description_variants: The 22B Nemotron-3 multi-turn-completion model's instruction-following SFT blend included OpenAssistant Conversations.
PPO RLHF stage: the 22B Nemotron-3 was optimised with PPO against a reward model trained on multi-turn preference pairs built from Open Assistant conversation trees.

edges.subject: Nemotron-3
edges.description: Assistant-turn completion fine-tuning of the 22B Nemotron-3 used HH-RLHF conversations, with a custom causal mask that exposes future user turns at the first turn position.

anchor_list.excerpt: The resulting model was fine-tuned for Assistant turn completion on conversations from the HH-RLHF dataset [2].
anchor_list.explanation: Pins HH-RLHF as the second-stage SFT corpus for the Assistant-turn completion model.
